channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration sues Chicago in latest crackdown on `sanctuary' cities
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Trump administration sued Chicago on Thursday alleging that 'sanctuary' laws in the nation's third-largest city “thwart" federal efforts to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers needed
item.description: President Trump on Thursday doubled down on his proposal for the U.S. to take control of the Gaza Strip and rebuild it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Megan Boswell trial: Defense scores win with admission of interview
item.description: BLOUNTVILLE, Tenn. (WJHL) -- Megan Boswell's defense scored a victory Thursday morning in her murder trial as Judge Jim Goodwin ruled they can cross-examine an investigator about an interview that saw Boswell admit to her baby Evelyn being dead 10 days before Evelyn’s body was found.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, NewsNation

item.title: Second employee dies after shooting at Ohio cosmetics warehouse and the suspect will be in court
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2nd victim dies after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility; suspect due in court Thursday
item.description: A second person has died after a gunman opened fire in a cosmetics facility in New Albany, Ohio, late on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, KREX 5

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: 5 universities facing federal probe over antisemitism
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher than white women around the time of childbirth in 2023, as maternal mortality fell below prepandemic levels overall but racial gaps widened, according to federal health data released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas school district warns parents Border Patrol may question or deport students
item.description: Parents across Texas are being warned that Border Patrol agents may be boarding school buses and their children may be detained or deported if they can not prove citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freezing hikers stuck in waist-deep snow rescued during Mount Washington whiteout
item.description: Two experienced hikers were rescued after an hourslong ordeal in which they became stranded in waist-deep snow and whiteout conditions on New Hampshire's challenging Mount Washington.
item.dc:creator: Kathy Mccormack And Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump is targeting antisemitism in schools. Experts fear other civil rights will be ignored
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal office that enforces civil rights at schools across the U.S. has been ordered to prioritize complaints of antisemitism above all else as it molds to President Donald Trump’s agenda, raising fears that other rights violations will go unpunished.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday intended to ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McConnell 'fine' after stumbling on Senate steps, using wheelchair as precaution
item.description: (AP) -- Sen. Mitch McConnell fell slightly on a few steps while exiting the Senate chamber Wednesday after late-morning votes. The Republican former leader was immediately scooped up by colleagues, Sen. Steve Daines, R-Mont., and Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CEO son of 'Silicon Valley pioneer' arrested in alleged San Francisco attack
item.description: A Silicon Valley CEO was arrested on Saturday in downtown San Francisco on suspicion of rape and other charges including robbery, assault with a deadly weapon, and false imprisonment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, WGN 9

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi to lead Justice Department
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday voted to confirm Pam Bondi as the next attorney general, giving her control of a Justice Department embroiled in controversy. The vote was 54-46. Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) joined all Republicans in voting in support of Bondi.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: 2 men facing charges following courtroom brawl in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – A routine court hearing devolved into chaos when two people in the gallery rushed at the defendant and ended up starting a brawl. Now, those two men are facing charges. It happened at a district court hearing Friday morning for murder suspect Alexander Ortiz.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration will consider redrawing boundaries of national monuments as part of energy push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As part of the Trump administration’s push to expand U.S. energy production, federal officials will review and consider redrawing the boundaries of national monuments created under previous presidents to protect unique landscapes and cultural resources.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims of the midair collision near DC recovered as NTSB probes altitude data
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — The remains of all 67 victims of last week's midair collision of an American Airlines flight and an Army helicopter near the nation's capital have been recovered, authorities said Tuesday. All but one has been identified.
item.dc:creator: SERKAN GURBUZ and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump picks former chief entangled in 'Sharpiegate' to lead NOAA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has nominated Neil Jacobs to lead the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, turning to the man who was his acting NOAA chief in 2019 when Trump altered an expected hurricane impact map in what became known as the “Sharpiegate” scandal.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suggests displaced Palestinians in Gaza be 'permanently' resettled outside war-torn territory
item.description: The renewed call by the Trump administration on Arab nations to relocate displaced Palestinians comes as Trump is set to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House later Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The man charged with stabbing Salman Rushdie is going on trial. The author will take the stand
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — In 2022, Salman Rushdie was about to deliver a lecture before a live audience in western New York when a man ran towards him and plunged a knife into the author’s hand as he raised it in self-defense.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why it's good for NASA that an asteroid carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the lunar far side.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's brings back iconic character to kick off Shamrock Shake season
item.description: McDonald's is bringing back another McDonaldland character for the first time in decades to kick off the return of Shamrock Shake season. The company announced Tuesday that Grimace will reunite with his Irish uncle, Uncle O’Grimacey, after stumbling upon his family’s original recipe for the shake.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: At least 20K have accepted Trump's government buyout offer
item.description: At least 20,000 federal employees have accepted what amounts to a buyout from the Trump administration, a source confirmed to The Hill on Tuesday. Employees still have until Feb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, WGN 9

item.title: Salvage crews recover engine, large portion of jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have recovered an engine and large pieces of fuselage and are working to retrieve a wing from the wreckage of a commercial airliner involved in last week's midair collision near Washington’s Reagan National Airport, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ACLU sues over Trump shutting down asylum access at the southern border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration advocacy groups on Monday sued the Trump administration over its ban on asylum access at the southern border, saying the sweeping restrictions illegally put people who are fleeing war and persecution in harm's way.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4
